evals.prompt: I want to build a skill that helps me write LinkedIn posts. I work in public sector tech consulting and post about digital transformation, AI adoption, and delivery methodology. I want the skill to match my tone - direct, no fluff, no hashtag spam. Can you help me create the skill from scratch?
evals.expected_output: A session that interviews for context, drafts a SKILL.md under 200 lines with a 3-part description, moves detail to references/ (each under 300 lines), and presents test prompts before running evals.
assertions: SKILL.md is produced and is under 200 lines
The description contains trigger, negative trigger, and outcome
At least one reference file is created
All reference files are under 300 lines each
Test prompts are presented to the user before any evals are run

evals.prompt: I have an existing skill for processing meeting notes. The SKILL.md is 235 lines long and it's getting slow - Claude seems to forget parts of the instructions by the end. Can you refactor it so it still works but loads faster? The file is at: /Users/jacqui/Library/CloudStorage/GoogleDrive-jacqui.read@madetech.com/My Drive/Vault/1. Projects/skill builder plus/skill-builder-plus-workspace/iteration-1/refactor-bloated-skill/meeting-notes-bloated-SKILL.md
evals.expected_output: A session that diagnoses the progressive disclosure violation, refactors SKILL.md to under 200 lines, creates reference files each under 300 lines, and performs a cold-start test.
assertions: The session correctly identifies the problem as SKILL.md being too long (bloat / progressive disclosure violation)
The refactored SKILL.md is under 200 lines
Content is moved to reference files, each under 300 lines
A cold-start test or line-count check is performed after refactoring

evals.prompt: I want to create a skill that generates commit messages from git diffs. It should follow conventional commits format (feat/fix/chore etc). I don't need evals for this one - just help me write a good skill quickly.
evals.expected_output: A session that respects the skip-evals request, produces a SKILL.md under 200 lines with a 3-part description and conventional commits examples.
assertions: The session respects the user's request to skip formal evals
SKILL.md is produced and is under 200 lines
The description contains trigger, negative trigger, and outcome
Conventional commits format examples are included (feat/fix/chore etc)

evals.prompt: I've written a skill that generates weekly project status reports from Jira data. I want to run evals on it. I've got 3 test prompts ready. Can you set up the eval workspace structure, explain exactly where each file goes, and show me what the subagent save-path instructions should look like?
evals.expected_output: A session that sets up a correctly structured eval workspace: eval directories named with eval- prefix, grading.json placed at run-1/grading.json (not outputs/grading.json), timing.json mentioned and placed at run-1/timing.json, and subagent prompts showing explicit output paths pointing to run-1/outputs/.
assertions: Eval directories are named with the eval- prefix (e.g. eval-jira-report, not jira-report)
grading.json is placed at <eval-dir>/<config>/run-1/grading.json, not inside outputs/
timing.json is mentioned and placed at <eval-dir>/<config>/run-1/timing.json
Subagent save-path instructions point to run-1/outputs/, not outputs/ directly
